user_message: Give me the manager view on this Contoso Ltd email thread. The customer escalated this morning.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 11:21 EST
Subject: URGENT: account health Issue at Contoso Ltd

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with account health. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
Date: 2024-02-02 17:04 EST
Subject: Re: Re: account health Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Angela Doyle <angela.doyle@contosoltd.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-09 11:46 EST
Subject: Re: account health Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with account health. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Contoso Ltd: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-M trap (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-M failure mode (No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-M -- No native 'cross-sell signal' primitive distinct from generic signal-to-opportun
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
